Committee.CommitteeName: Special Interim Committee on Property Tax Reform
Special Committee on Property Tax Reform

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 116-2, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative David Dolan, a Republican, proudly represents Scott County (District 148) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
A graduate of the University of Memphis School of Law, where he earned his Juris Doctor in 1981, Dolan brings a wealth of experience and a deep commitment to justice to the Missouri House, having served as a prosecuting attorney, associate circuit judge, and circuit judge in both Scott and Mississippi counties.
One of Dolan’s most notable achievements is his leadership in the establishment of Missouri's first juvenile drug court, an initiative that has had a lasting impact on the state’s approach to addressing substance abuse issues among youth. He also served as one of the first presidents of the Missouri drug courts, providing invaluable guidance to these vital programs. For his outstanding contributions to the legal and justice systems, he has received several prestigious awards, including the Missouri 2020 Judicial Excellence Award from the Missouri Supreme Court and the 2023 Mississippi County Law Enforcement Community Partner of the Year.
Outside of his legislative work, Dolan is an active member of St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church in Sikeston, where he engages in service and fellowship within his community. He enjoys spending time with his wife, Julie, and their two children, Win and Claire.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes and modifies provisions relating to court reporters
Increases the age from seventeen to eighteen as it relates to proceedings involving orders of protection
Provides that a grandparent shall be given priority if it is in the best interest of the child to be placed in the custody of a third party
Creates provisions relating to a post-consumer paint recycling program
Creates a new exemption from the earned paid sick time law
Allows graduates of certain programs to petition the court for limited driving privileges
Grants use of the House Chamber for swearing in of attorneys on Friday, April 25, 2025, and Friday, September 26, 2025, from 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Creates provisions relating to alternative therapies for veterans with PTSD and traumatic brain injuries
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Modifies provisions relating to the protection of children
Changes provisions relating to county coroners
